data.content: The file is generated successfully but never added to the compilation. The target needs to include the generated file in the `Compile` item group so Roslyn knows to compile it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The compiler doesn't know about the file because it's not included in the compilation.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` within the target that includes the generated file in the `Compile` items:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added after the file is written so the compiler picks it up.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target generated the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler didn't know about it.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="...">` item inside the target (after the file is written) so MSBuild includes the generated file in compilation.
